reviews.review_description: Please bring this back I’m begging I’ll do anything

Before I write the review I'd like to say that my review primarily focusses on the plutosphere service, not just the applab client.

I've been using plutosphere for over a year now and I can confidently say that this cloud gaming service is a game-changer for individuals who lack access to a high-end PC but still want the experience of PC VR gaming.

Perhaps one of the most impressive aspects of Plutosphere is the virtually nonexistent latency on the server side. For gaming and especially VR gaming that is really a must since it helps with the immersion and makes the gameplay smooth, responsive, and, most importantly, enjoyable. The machine itself is also capable of running almost any (vr) game you throw at it; that's also a great plus point. 

Plutosphere is not only great as a cloud gaming service but they also have a great community in the discord serving where people help each other, share games, and it’s overall just a great community for VR enthusiasts. In my experience, the support in the discord has been outstanding, with quick responses (most of the time) and effective solutions to any issues and questions. Beyond technical support in the discord, the staff at Plutosphere are also great. They’re always friendly, helpful, and they address problems fairly quickly.

While the price of Plutosphere is reasonable, It’s important to note that the service may not be the most cost-effective for users who engage in longer and more gaming sessions. For those who play for shorter durations, the pay as you go structure is great. However, for individuals who plan to spend significant amounts of time gaming and plan on using this for longer amounts of times per month it might find better value in alternatives offering monthly subscription plans.

Apparently this has a character limit so 
Overall I give plutosphere 4/5 stars since I think there is room for improvement Plutosphere is a great solution for anyone who’s looking to play PCVRgames on their standalone quest/pico

What the hell why is it not free bro you literally said it was free on Oculus quest 2 the app is free so why not just make it completely free like I don't get the point in this like what is the point like I can get long arms for like 2 days but like after that it's going to be like please pay your $30 now and then I get kicked off my Oculus I get bannd IN gorilla tag and you're making me a for it you're making me pay for getting banned

It's great playing PC games without needing a PC at all sometimes when I play games it actually feels like I'm playing and it has no lag but in the future I hope Plutosphere has mor improvements in the future

I admit, I found this app with low hopes thinking it would be laggy and low quality, but if you have good internet speeds it actually runs really well with good quality for a Quest. Also even though this app uses tokens that cost money their discord does frequent giveaways so even if you don’t have money at the time you can still play. Another thing is that even though steam VR is the main way to play you can install other games to the device like A Township Tale.Lastly if your internet cuts out frequently like mine don’t worry because it won’t disconnect the internet on the device it’ll just freeze you for a second until you reconnect.
Honesty haveing the experience to play pcvr games without a pc is crazy and only for 2$ thats a good deal

Overall plutosphere works very well. ive been using it for just over a year now and i havent really experienced that many problems with the app. what i would recommend though is that you have a good wifi connection

I have never had a problem in my time using Plutosphere, the app itself is very easy to use and the cloud machine runs very smoothly. The only problems I have ever encountered are SteamVR based, so not the fault of plutosphere. Overall I can highly recommend plutosphere to anyone wanting to try PCVR without a PC.

reviews.review_title: Best Thing I have found for vr.
reviews.review_description: I have always wanted to play games like Skyrim and Phasmophobia in vr and this app lets you do so with no input lag at all. The games run really well and allow you to play high quality vr games without owning a pc. I give it a 4/5 because it is really pricey but I guess that is unfair to score because it is the only one available.
